label: Stomach
quick_take: Stomach cancer has been falling globally for 50 years — the story of a disease chased down by refrigeration, falling *H. pylori* prevalence and better food hygiene. CI5 captures the decline with textbook clarity, but also the hold-out belts in East Asia and inland China.

findings.title: The Nordic collapse is near-total
findings.text: Iceland men 97.0 → 5.1 (−95 %), Finland 74.5 → 6.0 (−92 %), Norway 47.7 → 6.1 (−87 %), Denmark 40.6 → 7.1 (−83 %). Women in the same countries dropped 86–92 %. Together these constitute one of the most complete disappearances of a cancer burden ever recorded.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The Nordic collapse is near-total

findings.title: The Chinese belt matches the oesophagus belt — same counties
findings.text: Yangcheng males 168.9 (vol X), Shexian 151.9 (vol XI), Yanting 144.6 (vol XI), Cixian 118.0 (vol XI). 14–19× the world median. Three of these counties also top the oesophagus list — same rural diet signal (pickled vegetables, nitrosamines) hitting both sites.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The Chinese belt matches the oesophagus belt — same counties

findings.title: Hawaii Japanese shows the migrant inversion classic
findings.text: Inverted-U: 61.2 (vol I, Japan-imported rates) → peak 66.1 (vol II) → 7.7 (vol XII). Their origin rates in Japan are still falling too, but the Hawaii curve falls *faster* — environment dominates genetics.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Hawaii Japanese shows the migrant inversion classic

findings.title: Ugandan rise
findings.text: Kyadondo men 2.43 → 6.06 ASR (n=13→100) and women 1.08 → 4.55 (n=3→95). Small absolute numbers, and for men the crude rate actually falls slightly while ASR rises — direction is real but part of the signal is age-structure change. Pop_warning Uganda vol VII. Worth checking subsite (cardia vs non-cardia).
None of these findings rely on a coding break.
The Iceland/Finland baselines in vol I (1955–1965) may include some oesophageal cardia — but the magnitude is large enough that the story survives.
A "global Nordic-collapse" figure comparing Iceland / Finland / Norway / Denmark on a single chart would be striking.
Why is the decline slower (or absent) in Chinese rural registries? Pickled-food penetration, *H. pylori* prevalence, refrigeration access — a geographic story waiting to be told.
Uganda: is the rise driven by cardia adenocarcinoma (obesity / reflux signal) or non-cardia (*H. pylori*)?
